Launch Conversation
!
Our next strategy is the launch conversation.
!
This one might feel a little conceptual, but again we’re going to drill down on this as we
move forward.
!
I told you earlier that list building is the one thing that I stumbled onto that made all
the difference. Well, there’s one other thing. There are two things that have made all
the difference:
! • 	The first was the list building; always focus on the list and list relationship.
• 	The second one is focusing only selling what the market wants.
!
The way you figure out what the market wants is you tap into the conversation.
!
This has actually gotten a lot easier now with social media, because social media is just
one big long conversation. That has made it a lot easier. With launches you can
completely leverage that conversation.
!
If you had to sell something to someone, if your life depended on it, if your family’s
future depended on selling something to someone, the best most effective way to
convert someone to get them to buy is face-to-face.
!
You can sit down with someone and you can talk to them and judge whether they’re
starting to drift away, whether their eyes are glazing over, they’re not paying attention,
you can see what gets them excited and what turns them off.
!
That’s very powerful because now you’re having a conversation. You’re talking to them,
you’re judging their reaction. They can look at you, they can judge you, they can watch
your interest level. Very powerful.
!
The problem is it’s hard to leverage that. If you’re going to sell face-to-face, you can
only do that a few times a day.
!
Then on the other end of the spectrum the way to sell that has the most leverage is
probably TV, where you can just have millions of people see your message all at once.
On the other hand, you don’t have that interaction so you can’t tell what people are
thinking. You get massive leverage, but very little conversion.
!
The product launch with PLF allows a combination of those two. It gives you the big
leverage, maybe not as big as TV, but it gives you a lot of leverage as a one-to-many
type of media. But you’re able to get that conversation by people leaving comments,
the emails, and what they’re sharing in social media. It’s just a beautiful combination of
those two.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 2 of 4 --

That’s where this launch conversation is so strong. It lets you identify what the market
wants through that conversation, through that interaction, through surveys, comments,
social media. And it allows you to identify objections and hot points. It allows you to
identify what their desires are. This makes selling a lot easier.
!
A real key to creating that conversation is to direct it and to ask for it. In every one of
those prelaunch videos at the end of the video you ask for them to make a comment.
Or in between you might be sending out an email, you ask for interaction in the email.
You might have a P.S. like, “P.S. I’d love to know what you’re thinking. Just hit the reply
button right now and let me know.”
!
A lot of times you’ll say, “I’d love to know what you’re thinking,” but people just assume
that it’s a rhetorical statement that you don’t really mean it. But if you say, “P.S. I would
love to know what you think about this. Hit your reply button right now and send me an
email to tell me what you think.” Those are the magic words and you’ll get a lot more
interaction. It’s not like everyone is going to respond to you, or even 50%, but you will
get a lot more interaction that way.
!
Surveys are great for interaction. In your prelaunch content at the end of every video
say, “I’d love to know what you think. Let me know, scroll down and tell me what you
think in the comments.” Or even better instead of just “what you think,” give them
specific instructions. If you’re teaching them how to play a song on the guitar you could
say, “Scroll down and tell me what was the first song you learned on the guitar.” Ask
them a very specific question. The more specific you are in your request, the more
conversation, the more compliance, the more people are going to follow through and
leave a comment or reply to you.
!
Launch conversation is really the magic lever that sort of lies inside Product Launch
Formula.
!
Until you go through a launch, you don’t realize just how powerful this is. All of a
sudden as you go through prelaunch and you start to get all these comments back, you
can start to identify those objections, you can answer that in your next video.
!
Robert Collier, a famous copywriter, wrote about this long ago and he said,
!
“You want to enter the conversation in your prospect’s mind.”
!
You want to enter the conversation that’s already going on in their mind. The launch
conversation is how you tap into that conversation. You tap into what’s going on in their
head and then as you go forward your next piece of prelaunch content can address it,
can actually talk about what’s going on, what their objections are, what they’re excited
about.
!
The same thing with your sales video and your emails each step of the way, you’ve
entered the conversation in their mind. That’s why the launch conversation is so

powerful. Like I said, this is something that’s completely overlooked until the first time
you’re in a launch, that’s when you realize just how powerful this is.
